text: Brigitte is a versatile support hero who specializes in providing healing and overhealth to allies. Her primary value comes from being a bodyguard, providing protection to characters who are more susceptible to enemy ambushes than most. The large healing aura generated by Inspire makes her most useful when her team stays together. Despite her strength in close combat, she has little to no means of harming the enemy at range. Try to stay out of sight from enemy ranged dps until your tank engages. Her Shield Bash ability can be used to get in close with enemies. If hacked, she has almost no means of helping her team at all. Weapons & Abilities [ ]   Inspire (Passive): Brigitte's passive ability. Whenever she damages an enemy with Rocket Flail, Whip Shot or Shield Bash, a healing aura will be created around her which heals allies over time. Every attack will refresh the passive. Once an allied player has the effect of Inspire, they can leave the radius and still receive the healing until the duration expires. The ability has a short cooldown before it regenerates again. While the healing aura is very large, it has the weakest healing in terms of its raw numbers. Because the nature of the ability requires her to attack the enemy, this can sometimes be inconsistent when there is no enemy to attack. Keep as a group with your teammates, as the healing won't go through walls and centers around Brigitte.   Rocket Flail : Brigitte's primary weapon. Her primary fire is a melee attack with the flail. The attack is extended forward and can hit multiple enemies, although it deals low damage in contrast to its fast attack speed. Much like Reinhardt's Rocket Hammer, the Rocket Flail replaces Brigitte's melee attack. Unlike Reinhardt's   Rocket Hammer , Rocket Flail doesn't create any knockback to the enemy. It is best used in terms of an offensive tool rather than a defensive tool, especially combining with Shield Bash since she can rush to the enemy and combo them with the flail. Flail's rate of fire is faster than Inspire 's cooldown. This means that spamming Flail will not trigger Inspire every time. To maximize Inspire healing and self-protection, alternate Rocket Flail with Shield. This will allow you to heal yourself and your party and also survive to keep healing.   Repair Pack : When activated, Brigitte will throw a repair pack toward the targeted ally. When it contacts the target, it will heal a small amount of health in burst, and large amount of health over 2 seconds. This has a moderate cooldown, so it is best to use it on half to critical health allies, as if used wrong, it could cause important allies to be eliminated. There can also be many instances in which you can preemptively give a teammate a repair pack as they prepare to engage, allowing them to have a form of healing even when they leave Brigitte's line of sight. Prioritize this ability for characters with a lack of a self heal ability as it allows them to keep pressure on the enemy to begin a team fight. Using this ability when Inspire isn't active is recommended due to its large maximum range. Since most of the healing from this ability happens relatively slowly (over 2 seconds), if an ally is low health, you may need to follow up with shield-bash + shield to protect the ally while the healing takes place.   Whip Shot : When activated, Brigitte will throw her flail a long distance, dealing a decent amount of damage and knocking an enemy away. Her movement speed will be heavily decreased during the cast time. While it is a bit hard, this ability is useful to do an environmental kill when the enemy is standing around a cliff. Whip Shot creates a strong knockback compared to Shield Bash. You can increase the knockback from Shield Bash by activating Whip Shot immediately and sending the target off of a cliff or the objective. This ability also activates Inspire. This is useful as a kite tool to heal her teammates, as it has long range and short cooldown to compensate it. Try not to use it on an open area, as her reduced movement speed will make Brigitte easily headshot by the enemy. Her Rocket Flail has a very long second half animation; you can easily cancel this into a Whip Shot to increase your DPS. Brigitte is most vulnerable while using Whip Shot, as it cannot be cancelled with her shield. The animation duration varies depending on the distance at which the Whip Shot makes contact (if at all). The knockback is extremely useful at denying enemies such as Winston or Doomfist from taking high ground with their vertical mobility, as it stops the initial momentum from the vertical mobility cooldowns respectively.   Barrier Shield : When activated, Brigitte will deploy a frontal energy barrier to absorb any incoming damage. The barrier is only the size of Brigitte herself and can receive a limited amount of damage. Her movement speed will be slightly decreased when holding a shield. When not in use, the shield will begin to regenerate its health until it is redeployed. When broken, the shield can't be used for a short duration. Due to its small size, it is best to be used as a personal barrier or use it to protect a key teammate such as Mercy beginning   Resurrect , or to assist a Widow whilst she is scoped. Switch between primary fire and barrier mode more frequently when you are closing the gap with an enemy, as you can lure them to unleash a strong attack which the barrier can absorb. Much like Reinhardt's barrier, her Barrier Shield is extremely useful when playing mind games with strong enemy ultimates such as Reinhardt's   Earthshatter or Cassidy's   Deadeye . As long as her barrier is connected to the ground, it can block many different attacks that travel along the ground, such as Venture's Burrow emergence, or Doomfist's Meteor Strike. Brigitte's barrier can also block the damage from Orisa's Javelin Spin, however does not stop the knock back it causes.   Shield Bash : Only available while holding the barrier. When activated while holding the barrier, Brigitte will dash forward and damage an enemy in front of her. She will receive a short burst of movement increased during the cast time and has a slight delay after it contacts the target before it undeploys the shield. Shield Bash no longer stuns enemies in Overwatch 2 (except when ulting). Since this ability is only available with your barrier deployed, it is best to always leave a sliver of health on the barrier. This ability can also be used for quick movement towards or away from enemies. It can also be used to evade ultimates such as Mei's   Blizzard or Sigma's   Gravitic Flux when used to quickly dash outside the radius. In conjunction with Rocket Flail and Whip Shot, you can deal a large amount of damage before knocking the enemy away. Note that contacting an enemy with Shield Bash will have a slight delay before you can undeploy the shield. If Shield Bash collides with   Charge ,   Rocket Punch , a charging   B.O.B. , or another   Shield Bash , both participants get knocked down for 2 seconds.   Rally (Ultimate): Brigitte's ultimate ability. When activated, she will immediately receive 100 armor and generate an aura which teammates around her overhealth. The overhealth will be increased over time until reaching its cap or until destroyed. Only Brigitte moves faster while the ultimate is active. It also allows her shield bash to stun. You can use it to disrupt the enemy's channeled Ults. Her ultimate gives 300 overhealth over its duration, but is capped at 100 total over their health. This means if you use it out of combat to just generate overhealth for your team, you’re effectively only getting a third of its potential compared to having it running during combat. The speed boost Brigitte gains is very useful for her to get important melee hits in and to knockback key targets. Unlike Zenyatta's Transcendence and Lucio's Sound Barrier, her ultimate is more proactive, as it's often better to use it a little bit ahead of a big fight so it has some time to stack up. Since there is 15 overhealth given per half-second tick, it takes 3 seconds to replenish 90 overhealth. General Strategies [ ] Brigitte is a great addition to a team, but should not be a solo healer or even paired with Lúcio . Brigitte is a great substitute for Zenyatta . Her Rocket Flail is a great weapon to attack many enemies. Whip Shot is useful for knocking enemies off a cliff, or keeping them off a choke point. Whip Shot is quick, but still requires Brigitte to lead her shot carefully. Be careful when using Whip Shot, as the time it takes to retract can leave Brigitte vulnerable. The speed decrease can make her an easy target for snipers. Brigitte's Barrier Shield is a good defense for protecting herself from low-health enemies, and can block some Ultimates entirely. Shield Bash combined with a melee and Whip Shot can do massive damage quickly before knocking the target away. Brigitte should always help friendly healers, especially when they are being harassed by flankers. In addition, use your shield to cover Mercy while she is resurrecting a teammate. Rally can be a push tool or a protection tool. Her Repair Pack is a great way to heal allies, but be wary of its cooldown. Try to avoid Sombra , as Brigitte's abilities are almost entirely her source of usefulness. However, unlike Lucio, her passive still activates while hacked. While she is labeled as a support hero, Brigitte is somewhat of a Tank/Support hybrid. She has the highest amount of total health points among Support heroes (50 armor/200 HP), and her Barrier Shield can be a useful damage blocker. There are three main threats to Brigitte: Heroes that can output a high amount of damage quickly (such as Junkrat or Bastion), snipers (as she has a larger hit-box than other supports), and Tanks due to their high damage output at close range and high health pools. Be wary of these when playing, as they can easily take her out. Similar to Reinhardt's Rocket Hammer, Brigitte's Rocket Flail can do damage to multiple enemies around her. PC and *Console players can quickly swing their aim around while her attack animation is playing ("*" = Sensitivity value above 85% is required). Between her standard Rocket Flail attacks, Shield Bash, and Whip Shot, Brigitte is capable of devastating burst damage that can take her opponent off-guard and kill them before they can react. Low-health heroes such as Tracer, Moira, and Genji are her best potential targets. While it's tempting to use Whip Shot as the final move of a combo, it's important to be aware of whether or not your opponent will survive the attack. If they do, they'll be sent flying away from you and your team, making it easy for them to escape and recover. In this situation, it's best to position yourself so Whip Shot will knock your opponent into a desirable location (such as towards your team or off a cliff), or if necessary, or refrain from using Whip Shot altogether and try to finish them off with standard attacks.
